description_long: This is a tall, multi-story office building characterized by a series of stacked, offset rectangular floor plates, creating a visually dynamic and tiered facade. The exterior features a repetitive pattern of horizontal bands, likely representing balconies or sunshades, interspersed with large window sections. The roof is flat and includes a central circular structure, possibly a mechanical penthouse or rooftop amenity, as well as landscaped areas and access points. The building's base is slightly wider, suggesting a lobby or public entrance area, and the overall color palette is dominated by shades of gray, white, and muted earth tones. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from concrete and glass, with metal elements for railings and facade details. The design is modern, with a focus on geometric forms and functional aesthetics.
description: A modern, multi-story office building with stacked, offset floors, horizontal bands, and large window sections, featuring a flat roof with a central circular structure and landscaped areas.
description_view_0: Front view shows the tiered, offset floor plates and a prominent base, with visible balconies and window bands.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the horizontal layering and the central circular rooftop structure, with more pronounced facade depth.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the continuation of the offset floor pattern and window bands, with less detail at the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors the horizontal banding and rooftop features, emphasizing the building's symmetry and massing.
attribute_window_pattern: Regular, horizontal bands of large rectangular windows alternating with solid facade elements, repeated consistently across all floors.
